chapter_title.ar: باب السُّنَّةِ فِي لُبْسِ الْخُفِّ وَ النَّعْلِ وَ خَلْعِهِمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 20 – The Sunnah regarding wearing of the socks, and the sandals, and taking them off

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/353)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/353)
text: هُ كَانَ يَمْشِي فِي نَعْلٍ وَاحِدَةٍ وَ يُصْلِحُ الْأُخْرَى لَا يَرَى بِذَلِكَ بَأْساً .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, from Ali<sup>asws</sup>, if one were to walk in one shoe and repair the other, he<sup>asws</sup> did not see any problem with that’.
en.sarwar: 6. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from al-Nawfaliy from al-Sakuniy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that Ali ibn al-Husayn, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, walked with one shoe while repairing the other one, and he did not consider it harmful.’”
